design_philosophy.core_loop: Select team, compete in tournament bracket format, progress through sequential matches to championship
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Win-loss outcomes in turn-based baseball matches; manage team selection and gameplay execution across tournament structure
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead a Little League team to championship victory; experience authentic Little League Baseball competition
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commitment to authentic Little League ruleset (7-inning games in US version); regional design choices emphasize different cultural baseball traditions (Japanese vs. American ceremony/traditions)

difficulty.checkpointing: Password system in US version enables progress saves between matches
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Team selection likely affects match difficulty; tournament bracket structure provides fixed progression path

progression.unlock_mechanism: Tournament bracket advancement; defeat opponents to progress toward championship
replayability_hooks: Different tournament outcomes based on team choice
Password system enables replaying specific tournament stages

world_building_techniques: Opening ceremony sequences (more elaborate in Japanese version)
Team selection menus with distinct team identities
Game-over and ending sequences with regional variations

faction_indicators: Tournament bracket shows competing teams as rivals
danger_signals: Championship opponents represent escalating competitive threats
environmental_storytelling: Baseball field layouts (sandlot in Japanese version has larger sand area)
Opening ceremony atmosphere establishes competitive context

sources_used: tcrf: Little League Baseball: Championship Series - The Cutting Room Floor
